fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 66 uses strict greater-than (`>`) instead of greater-than-or-equal (`>=`), so when `len(recent) == limit` the function incorrectly allows the request and returns remaining=0 instead of denying it.

constraints.intent: When len(recent) equals limit the branch should be taken (deny), so the condition len(recent) > limit is FALSE at this point — flagging the off-by-one bug.
If the request is allowed (else branch), remaining must be strictly positive; when len(recent)==limit this is violated, exposing the off-by-one.
Sanity check that the boundary case len(recent)==limit is handled by the subsequent condition using >= rather than >.
The result (True, 0) is logically inconsistent — allowed but no remaining capacity — and should never be returned.
